content.full_text:
State of California Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • • Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660        322-5901        322-6441        322-6444

July 7, 1978

TO: ALL JUNE 6 PRIMARY ELECTION STATE CANDIDATES

FROM: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE AND ANALYSIS DIVISION

SUBJECT: POST-ELECTION CAMPAIGN STATEMENTS

All candidates for State office on the June 6 primary ballot, including judges, and their campaign committees are required to file post-election campaign statements no later than Monday, July 31.

Several candidates and committees committed errors in their pre-election campaign statements which should be avoided in post-election statements and any future statements. Some of the most common errors included:

• The failure to fully and completely identify the sources of contributions. The full name, street address, occupation and name of employer, or the principal place of business if self-employed, must be stated for anyone from whom a contribution or contributions totaling $50 or more has been received.

• The failure to list the date when a contribution or contributions totaling $50 or more are received.

• The failure to cumulate contributions. All contributions received from a single source must be cumulated (combined) since the closing date of the last post-election campaign statement filed. Since the July 31 statement is a post-election statement, the cumulation process will begin anew on August 1 for candidates on the November ballot.

• The failure to itemize expenditures made through advertising or public relations firms. Expenditures made by an agent or independent contractor, such as an advertising or public relations agency, must be reported by the candidate or committee as if they spent the funds directly. The only exception is if the advertising agency or public relations firm files a separate campaign statement itemizing the expenditures.

Please discuss these matters with your campaign treasurer or whomever prepares your campaign statements. Minor errors can be corrected with amended statements, but serious and repeated disclosure gaps in statements can lead to enforcement actions.

If you have any questions or need help, please contact the Technical Assistance and Analysis Division in Sacramento at (916) 322-5662 or in Los Angeles at (213) 620-5196.
